name: Krumkake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium mixing bowl, beat eggs with an electric mixer on medium speed for about 1 minute.
2. Add sugar; beat until the sugar is almost dissolved, about 3 minutes.
3. Melt butter and let cool slightly. Stir the melted butter into the egg mixture.
4. Add flour, vanilla or almond extract, and nutmeg; stir just until smooth.
5. Heat a Krumkake iron on the range-top over medium-low heat.
6. Spoon about 1 tablespoon of the batter onto the hot, ungreased iron. Close gently but firmly.
7. Cook for about 30 seconds or until light golden brown. Turn the iron and cook for another 30 seconds.
8. Open the iron carefully. Loosen the cookie with a narrow spatula; invert onto a wire rack.
9. Immediately roll the cookie into a cone or cylinder using a wooden or metal form.
10. Let cool around the cone or cylinder until the cookie holds its shape.
11. Reheat the iron and repeat with remaining batter.
12. Cool rolled cookies on a wire rack. If desired, pipe or fill with whipped cream; top with chopped almonds or chocolate-flavored sprinkles, or serve with preserves if desired. Makes 26-30 cookies.
